title: Monday Recovery Endurance Check
objective: Restart bike rhythm after a weekend with no riding but meaningful cottage manual work.
why_now: The missed Saturday ride is deleted, not carried as debt. The 2h hike, tree cutting, and trench digging count as real fatigue context, so Monday should check legs/back and restore easy bike frequency before Tuesday volume and Wednesday sweet spot.

targets.notes: 60 minutes easy endurance at 52-66% FTP. Keep pressure light; this is a readiness check, not make-up training.

warmup.notes: Start very easy and check legs/back.

steps.notes: Easy endurance only. Smooth cadence, low force, no tempo.

nutrition.pre: Normal meal timing is enough. If hungry, add 20-30g carbs 30-60 min before.
nutrition.post: Normal meal with 25-35g protein and a normal carb serving.
nutrition.centr_recipe: No new Centr link needed; use a normal recovery plate.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: none required beyond normal fluids. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Easy walking only if it loosens the back/hips.
recovery.evening: No extra lower-body or heavy yard work if Wednesday sweet spot is still the goal.
recovery.next_morning_check: Tuesday volume proceeds if legs/back feel normal and sleep is acceptable.

skill_focus.action: Keep cadence smooth and torque low.
skill_focus.why: This unloads back/hips after digging and keeps the ride useful without adding fatigue.

mental_focus.why: A clean easy ride restores bike frequency without pretending the missed weekend ride is debt.
mental_focus.reset: If you chase speed, shift easier for two minutes and let the ride stay boring.

execution_notes.if_too_hard: Cut to 45 minutes easy spin or stop if back/legs feel worse after 15 minutes.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Finish looser or the same, not more tired.
